title: citrus decumana var. paradisi
intro: Citrus decumana var. paradisi, also known as the pummelo, is a large grapefruit-like citrus fruit that belongs to the Rutaceae family. The pummelo has been used for its medicinal properties, including its high vitamin C content and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. The health benefits of the pummelo include its ability to aid in digestion, lower cholesterol levels, and provide antioxidant properties that protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of pummelo include juice, tea, and extracts, which can be used to make traditional remedies for various health issues, including digestive problems and fever reduction.
